title: Ocotea marowynensis
taxonomy: Ocotea marowynensis is classified within the plant family Lauraceae, a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as laurels. This family is part of the order Laurales, which is a distinct group of plants that are often characterized by their aromatic leaves and fruits. The Lauraceae family is further divided into several subfamilies and tribes, with Ocotea marowynensis being a member of the tribe Laureae. The tribe Laureae is a large and complex group that includes many species of evergreen trees and shrubs. Ocotea marowynensis is a specific species within the tribe Laureae, and its classification reflects its unique characteristics and relationships to other plants within the family.

parts: Ocotea marowynensis is native to the Amazon rainforest and its medicinal properties have been studied for its potential health benefits. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots are the primary medicinal parts, which are traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions. The bark contains high levels of sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. intro: Ocotea marowynensis, a species of Ocotea, is a type of tree native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the family Lauraceae. The essential oil extracted from Ocotea marowynensis has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including stress, anxiety, and insomnia, due to its calming and sedative properties. The plant is rich in bioactive constituents, including ocotea acetate, limonene, and alpha- and beta-pinene, which are known for their antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities. The most common herbal preparations made with Ocotea marowynensis include essential oils, capsules, and teas, often used to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
